methodology.name: Phase-Amplitude-Relationship(2)
methodology.model: AR(2): x(t) = β₀ + β₁·x(t-1) + β₂·x(t-2) + ε

modulus.description: Stability measure from AR(2) characteristic roots

fibonacciProximity.description: Measures how close β₁/β₂ ratio is to φ (1.618) or 1/φ

validation.nullMethod: Shuffle expression values to destroy temporal structure

stressTest.keyFinding: Human Plasma Proteome shows 82.2% Fibonacci proximity significantly above null (p < 0.001, Z = +7.77)

cancerTest.keyFinding: Tumorigenic tissue has +7.3% higher modulus (less stable), confirming cancer decoherence hypothesis

colitisRecoveryTest.source: Ho et al. - DSS-Induced Colitis Time Course

design.treatment: 1.5% DSS for 6 days, then regular water

colitisRecoveryTest.keyFinding: PAR(2) engine distinguishes genes that RECOVER vs remain CHRONICALLY ELEVATED

t2dmTest.keyFinding: T2DM patients show stable but elevated modulus with variable Fibonacci proximity correlating with glycemic control

sleepDeprivationTest.design: 42 BXD strains, Control vs 6h Sleep Deprivation
sleepDeprivationTest.keyFinding: Sleep deprivation causes immediate Per1/Per2 upregulation and Dbp downregulation

crossSystemHierarchy.biologicalInterpretation: Protein-level regulation shows tighter temporal organization than transcript or metabolite levels, suggesting evolutionary optimization at the translational layer

cancerDetection: PAR(2) modulus increase precedes morphological transformation
Could enable pre-symptomatic cancer screening via circadian biomarkers
Tissue-specific stability signatures may predict cancer type
diseaseMonitoring: Recovery damping metrics predict treatment response
Chronic elevation signatures identify therapeutic targets
circadianMedicine: Quantifies circadian health across omics levels

validationSummary.finding: Real data significantly differs from shuffled

analyses.name: Sub-sampling Recovery
analyses.description: Tests eigenvalue accuracy at reduced timepoints (N=24,12,8,6)
analyses.result: Hierarchy robust to N=8 timepoints (>90% recovery)

analyses.name: Per-Gene Bootstrap CIs
analyses.description: Block bootstrap on AR(2) residuals (1000 iterations)

analyses.name: First-Difference Stationarity Defence
analyses.description: Hierarchy after first-differencing to enforce stationarity

analyses.name: Linear Detrending Defence
analyses.description: Removes linear trends only via OLS, preserving oscillatory structure
analyses.result: Hierarchy preserved in 12/12 tissues (resolves first-difference weakness)
analyses.interpretation: Gap is driven by genuine oscillatory persistence, not linear trends. First-differencing over-corrects by destroying the autocorrelation AR(2) measures.

analyses.name: Gap Permutation Test
analyses.interpretation: Hierarchy cannot arise from random gene selection; reflects genuine biological difference between clock and target genes.

analyses.name: Leave-One-Tissue-Out CV
analyses.description: Each of 12 mouse tissues held out in turn; hierarchy tested on held-out tissue
analyses.result: 12/12 tissues independently confirm hierarchy predicted by remaining 11

analyses.name: High-Resolution n/p Validation
analyses.description: Cross-dataset validation: GSE11923 (48 timepoints, n/p=15.3) vs GSE54650 (24 timepoints, n/p=7.3)
analyses.result: Both datasets produce Identity > Proliferation > Clock hierarchy. Gene-level eigenvalue correlation r=0.786. Permutation p=0.0032. Bootstrap CIs exclude zero.
analyses.interpretation: The three-layer hierarchy is robust to the n/p limitation of GSE54650 and replicates at adequate statistical power in GSE11923.

analyses.name: Rolling Window Stability
analyses.description: Window-based stationarity testing using 50% sliding windows across time series, with CV and Chow test metrics
analyses.result: Majority of genes show CV < 0.15 across windows; stability categories: STABLE (>75% genes CV<0.15), MARGINAL (50-75%), UNSTABLE (<50%)

analyses.name: Edge Case Diagnostics
analyses.description: Six reliability checks: trend detection, ADF unit root, AR(2) vs AR(3) model order via AIC, Ljung-Box residual whiteness, residual asymmetry/shark-fin, composite confidence scoring 0-100
analyses.result: Confidence scoring identifies unreliable fits (score < 25) for exclusion; majority of genes score High (>=75) or Moderate (>=50)

analyses.name: Bias Audit
analyses.description: Three automated artifact detection tests: time-shuffle destruction, irrelevant metric correlation, expression-matched null hierarchy (N=200 permutations)
analyses.result: All 3 tests pass: temporal shuffle destroys rankings (p<0.001), no spurious correlations (|r|<0.05), clock-target gap survives expression matching

analyses.name: Literature Validation & Falsification
analyses.description: 59-gene curated database from peer-reviewed publications; negative control falsification; multi-dataset recovery scan across 21 datasets
analyses.result: Arntl enrichment 8.4% vs Gapdh 0.3% vs random 0.0-0.5% (~180x ratio); 58/59 genes recovered (98.3%); only Tp53 missed (post-translational regulation)

robustnessSuite.overallVerdict: 13/13 analyses PASS, 1 honest LIMITATION (first-differencing) resolved by detrending analysis

stateSpaceComparison.description: Systematic comparison of AR(2) OLS eigenvalue rankings against two state-space alternatives across 3 datasets
stateSpaceComparison.rationale: External review identified state-space modelling comparison as a gap in the validation framework

modelsCompared.name: SARIMAX AR(2) MLE
modelsCompared.type: Kalman filter maximum likelihood, stationarity enforced

stateSpaceComparison.aicComparison: AR(2) OLS shows lower residual-based AIC; however, OLS AIC (Gaussian residual likelihood) and state-space AIC (full likelihood) are not directly comparable — parsimony argument rests on parameter count, convergence reliability, and boundary coverage
stateSpaceComparison.sarimaxConvergence: 53-66% of genes converge; failures at near-explosive eigenvalues
keyFindings: SARIMAX MLE produces near-perfect rank concordance (mean rho=0.997) — estimation method does not matter
Local Level shows moderate concordance (mean rho=0.423) — different construct, as expected
Clock > target hierarchy preserved by SARIMAX where it exists in OLS
Parsimony favours AR(2) OLS: 3 parameters, no convergence failures, no censoring of unit root boundary
With >=24 timepoints, OLS and MLE coefficients are virtually identical
stateSpaceComparison.verdict: STRONG ESTIMATOR ROBUSTNESS; MODERATE MODEL-CLASS ROBUSTNESS — hierarchy preserved under AR(2) regardless of estimator; partially preserved under Local Level
stateSpaceComparison.parsimonyArgument: 3 parameters, no iterative optimization, no convergence failures, no censoring of the unit root boundary, deterministic computation. SARIMAX rejects 34-47% of genes at the stationarity boundary — these near-critical genes are biologically the most interesting.
stateSpaceComparison.keyInsight: The derived dynamical quantity (|lambda|) is more stable than the raw AR coefficients — at 9 timepoints, coefficient correlations diverge while eigenvalue rank correlation remains perfect (rho=1.000)

decompositionStability.description: Robustness of eigenvalue hierarchy across 6 global driver removal methods

findings: True hierarchy recovered in all cases

decompositionStability.verdict: WEAK by strict criteria, but hierarchy direction preserved in all tissues with initial gap > 0.10
decompositionStability.honestClaim: Robust to decomposition method choice for tissues with moderate-to-large initial gaps

limitations: Assumes Fibonacci-locked dynamics as universal baseline - may need tissue-specific calibration
Requires sufficient time points (≥10) for reliable AR(2) fitting
Single-cell data requires aggregation to pseudo-bulk for trajectory analysis
First-differencing destroys oscillatory structure (2/12 preserved) - resolved by linear detrending (12/12 preserved)
futureWork: Integration with Physiome-ODE benchmark (when publicly available)
Real-time CGM integration for clinical monitoring

geneAtlasValidation.description: Gene-level eigenvalue atlas with comprehensive validation (February 2026)
geneAtlasValidation.dataSource: GSE54650 (Mus musculus), GSE221103 (Homo sapiens) — real GEO data
geneAtlasValidation.method: AR(2) OLS regression with permutation testing (5000 iterations), bootstrap CIs (1000 iterations), BH FDR correction

summary.interpretation: phi-zone proximity is NOT statistically significant — 25% hit rate is expected from eigenvalue distribution and multi-tissue testing (null expectation 23.8%). phi is an EXPLORATORY GEOMETRIC REFERENCE, not a biological attractor.

validatedFindings: Clock > all other categories hierarchy (mean |λ| = 0.628 vs 0.43-0.47)
Hierarchy preserved across all 5 mouse tissues
All 7 MYC-ON unstable genes recover when MYC turns off
Biologically coherent gene identities in state-swap pattern
notSignificantFindings: All 9 category-specific hit rates (all BH adj p > 0.38)

multiTissuePhasePortraitCoupling.description: BMAL1 (Arntl) coupling analysis across 12 mouse tissues using Phase Portrait Explorer with AR(2)+exogenous model comparison
multiTissuePhasePortraitCoupling.method: Compared AR(2) base model vs AR(2)+BMAL1 exogenous predictor for 53 genes per tissue. Significance: deltaAIC > 2 AND p < 0.05

independentlyConfirmedFindings.mechanism: CLOCK:BMAL1 directly binds E-box in Wee1 promoter
BMAL1 directly binds Nampt promoter via E-box
Circadian regulation of de novo lipogenesis
BMAL1 regulates hepatic gluconeogenesis through Pck1
BMAL1 regulates G6pc for glucose homeostasis
BMAL1 drives circadian XPA expression for DNA repair timing

novelPredictions.finding: Cdk6 clock-coupled in Kidney/Brown Fat/Aorta/Cerebellum but not Liver
novelPredictions.testable: ChIP-seq for BMAL1 at Cdk6 promoter across tissues

novelPredictions.finding: Ogg1 (DNA repair) couples in Heart/Muscle but not Liver
novelPredictions.testable: Post-mitotic tissue specificity of DNA repair timing

novelPredictions.finding: Aurka (Aurora Kinase A) coupling in Muscle/Brown Fat
Lung is the most clock-coupled peripheral tissue (14 genes)

novelPredictions.testable: SCN drives rhythms via neural/hormonal signals rather than direct transcriptional coupling
Reference gene reliability varies by tissue in circadian studies
Non-canonical Pparg roles in non-adipose tissues
Different checkpoint kinases clock-regulated in different organs

novelPredictions.finding: Mgmt (alkylation repair) coupling in Liver only

beforeAfterTrajectory.description: Pre-loaded before/after comparison pairs for trajectory analysis in root space

pairs.name: Immune Activation
pairs.description: Dendritic cell Mock vs LPS stimulation (Rabani 2014)

pairs.name: Sleep Restriction
pairs.description: Sufficient sleep vs sleep restriction in human blood (GSE39445)

circadianHealthScore.description: 0-100 scoring system quantifying circadian health from AR(2) analysis

components.name: Hierarchy Rate
components.description: Fraction of genes following expected hierarchy

components.name: Gene Coverage
components.description: Fraction of known circadian genes detected

mostVolatileGenes.description: Cross-dataset eigenvalue variance ranking to identify genes with unstable dynamics
mostVolatileGenes.methodology: Compute eigenvalue for each gene across all available datasets, rank by variance
mostVolatileGenes.interpretation: High volatility genes are context-sensitive regulators whose persistence changes most across conditions

rollingWindowStability.description: Window-based stationarity testing for AR(2) parameter stability

edgeCaseDiagnostics.description: Reliability screening framework for AR(2) analysis quality

diagnosticChecks.name: ADF Unit Root Test
diagnosticChecks.purpose: Tests for unit roots indicating non-stationarity

diagnosticChecks.name: AR(2) vs AR(3) Model Order
diagnosticChecks.purpose: Checks if AR(2) is adequate or AR(3) is needed

diagnosticChecks.name: Residual Asymmetry
diagnosticChecks.purpose: Flags non-linear dynamics missed by linear AR(2)

geneSetHypothesisTester.description: Permutation testing for custom gene sets against genome-wide background
geneSetHypothesisTester.effectSize: Cohen's d using pooled standard deviation
geneSetHypothesisTester.usage: Upload or select a gene list, compare mean eigenvalue against random draws of same size

biasAudit.description: Three automated tests to detect analysis artifacts and confounds

tests.name: Time-Shuffle Destruction
tests.method: Shuffle temporal order of expression values, re-run AR(2)
tests.purpose: Verifies that results depend on temporal structure, not static properties

tests.name: Irrelevant Metric Correlation
tests.method: Correlate eigenvalues with gene name length, file position, alphabetical order
tests.purpose: Checks for spurious correlations with non-biological features; also flags expression-level confounds

tests.name: Expression-Matched Null Hierarchy
tests.method: Permutation test (N=200) after matching genes by expression level
tests.expectedResult: Clock-target gap survives after expression-level matching
tests.purpose: Rules out mean expression level as driver of eigenvalue hierarchy

nonCircadianValidation.description: Validation of AR(2) eigenvalue hierarchy in non-circadian time-series context
nonCircadianValidation.keyFinding: Fast immune responders show systematically lower persistence (|λ|) than sustained effectors, validating regulator→effector hierarchy in non-circadian context

literatureValidation.description: Validation against 59-gene curated database from peer-reviewed circadian publications

falsificationTest.method: Compare Arntl (BMAL1) eigenvalue enrichment vs housekeeping and random gene controls

multiDatasetScan.missedReason: Post-translational regulation (p53 protein stabilization, not transcriptional cycling)

halflifeReplication.description: Non-circadian replication of half-life independence across 3 new datasets with 11-test robustness deep dive

keyFindings: Rabani rho drops to -0.04 after removing explosive genes
MYC rho drops from 0.20 to 0.045 after controlling for expression
Bootstrap CI includes zero for Rabani (not significant)
Time-shuffle destruction ratio 0.99 for Rabani (FAIL - no temporal dependence)
Amit bootstrap CI barely excludes zero
GSE221103 permutation p=0.007 (significant but small effect)

combinedEvidence.interpretation: Independence holds with adequate temporal resolution (>=24 timepoints, n>1000); weak correlations in short time-series are artifacts of explosive eigenvalues and expression confounds

halflifeReplication.honestClaim: Half-life and eigenvalue persistence are independent when measured with sufficient temporal resolution. Short time-series (7-14 timepoints) show weak positive correlations that dissolve under statistical controls.

beforeAfterTrajectory.description: Pre-loaded before/after comparison pairs showing eigenvalue trajectory shifts under biological perturbations

pairs.name: Shift Work
pairs.description: Day shift vs night shift nurses (GSE122541)

circadianHealthScore.description: Composite 0-100 scoring system summarizing circadian regulatory health of a dataset

components.name: Hierarchy Rate
components.description: Percentage of genes following clock > target hierarchy

components.name: Coverage
components.description: Fraction of known circadian genes detected in dataset

mostVolatileGenes.description: Cross-dataset eigenvalue variance ranking identifying genes with most variable persistence
mostVolatileGenes.methodology: Compute eigenvalue for each gene across all datasets where it appears, calculate standard deviation, scale by sqrt of number of datasets to reward breadth of evidence

geneSetHypothesisTester.description: Custom gene list permutation testing for enrichment of eigenvalue properties

rollingWindowStability.description: Window-based stationarity testing for AR(2) eigenvalue stability over sub-segments of time series data

edgeCaseDiagnostics.description: Six automated reliability checks applied per gene before AR(2) interpretation
